zh.Moulsford, Berkshire, England: 英國波克夏姆爾斯福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Talbot於大約1295年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot20歲，母親Anne Boteler24歲。她於大約1307年與Sir John Carru在英國英格蘭賀福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1335年享年41歲。

zh-hans.Moulsford, Berkshire, England: 英国波克夏姆爾斯福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Talbot于大约1295年出生在英国賀福爾时，她的父亲Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot20岁，母亲Anne Boteler24岁。她于大约1307年与Sir John Carru在英国英格兰賀福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1335年享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Talbot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Marcel, Anatole, Armand, Aubert, Camille, Gilles, Normand, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Auguste.
English and Irish (of Norman origin); French: from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Talebot, a pet form of Old French Talebert, Talbert (see Talbert 1). Compare Talbert 2.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Talbald, composed of the elements dal ‘bright, proud’ or tal ‘valley’ + bald ‘bold’.

variants.name: Talbert
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin); French: from the Old French personal name Talebert, Talbert, from ancient Germanic Talaberht or Dalbert, composed of the elements dal ‘bright, proud’ or tal ‘valley’ + berht ‘bright’.
Altered form of English   Talbot  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Talbott
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Talbot  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Talbot was born about 1295, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot, was 20 and her mother, Anne Boteler, was 24. She married Sir John Carru about 1307, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1335, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Talbot est née environ 1295, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Anne Boteler, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Carru environ 1307, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1335, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Talbot um 1295 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot 20 und ihre Mutter Anne Boteler 24. Sie hat um 1307 in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Carru geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1335 im Alter von 41 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Talbot è nata il circa 1295 nel Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Anne Boteler, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Carru circa 1307, nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1335, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Talbotが約1295年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbotは20歳，母Anne Botelerは24歳であった。約1307年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでSir John Carruと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1335年に41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Talbot(이)가 대략 1295년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Boteler(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1307년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 Sir John Carru(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1335년 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Talbot nasceu aproximadamente 1295, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Anne Boteler, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Carru aproximadamente 1307, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1335, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Talbot родилась около 1295 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Boteler, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Carru около 1307 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1335 г. в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Talbot nació aproximadamente 1295, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Gilbert Talbot 1st Lord Talbot, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Anne Boteler, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Sir John Carru aproximadamente 1307, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1335, a la edad de 41 años.
